messages.content:
In "My grandma" by Letty Cottin Pogrebin. The internal conflict regarding Grandma is that the narrator-
A.secretly wants to be more Grandma

B.wants Grandma to love her but fears rejection

C.is embarrassed by Grandma's words and actions

D.worries that she isn't living up to Grandma's expectations
B. wants Grandma to love her but fears rejection

The first half of the story MOSTLY-
A. explain events leading up to the time of the assembly

B. tell about Grandma's experiences in the Old Country

C. explain why the school is part of An Oral History Project

D. predict how the narrator's life will change after the assembly
A. explain events leading up to the time of the assembly.

The first half of the story MOSTLY-

A. explain events leading up to the time of the assembly

B. tell about Grandma's experiences in the Old Country

C. explain why the school is part of An Oral History Project

D. predict how the narrator's life will change after the assembly
A. explain events leading up to the time of the assembly.

At first, the narrator MOSTLY bases her opinion of Grandma on-

A. Mom's insight

B. Grandma's stories

C. her own impressions

D. her friends' reactions
C. her own impressions.

Read this sentence from the story
"I had to get down on my knees and beg her to shut up so my grandmother wouldn't hear and get her feelings hurt."
What character trait does this reveal about the narrator?
Group of answer choices

bravery

compassion

determination

curiosity
compassion.

What effect does point of view have on the story?

Readers see things only through Grandma's eyes

Readers do not know the feelings of the characters

Readers learn the narrator's private thoughts and feelings

Readers see things only as the narrator's friends see them
Readers learn the narrator's private thoughts and feelings.

Which event MOST influences the narrator's change in perspective?
Group of answer choices

the decision to nominate Grandma for the Grammy

the decision to choose Grandma for the Oral History Project Award

the story about Grandma that the narrator had not heard

the success of Grandma's assembly presentation
the success of Grandma's assembly presentation.

What di the Statue of Liberty symbolize to Grandma when she first saw it?
Group of answer choices

safety

honor

courage

pride
courage.

What did the Statue of Liberty symbolize to Grandma when she first saw it?
Group of answer choices

safety

honor

courage

pride
courage.

What does the narrator find out that she has in common with her Grandmother?
Group of answer choices

They were both born in the same country

They prefer to eat the same types of food

They both felt ashamed of their grandmothers

They shared similar childhood experiences
They shared similar childhood experiences.

At the end of the story, readers can infer that-
Group of answer choices

the narrator will be more understanding of Grandma from now on

the narrator's relationship with Grandma will not change at all

the narrator's friends will continue to make fun of Grandma

Grandma will learn that the narrator is unhappy with her
the narrator will be more understanding of Grandma from now on.

Which of the following BEST expresses a theme of the story?
Group of answer choices

importance of honesty in families

rewards of volunteerism in the community

value of friendships at school

complexity of generational relationships
complexity of generational relationships.
